choice.reasoning: Use the strongest identifier-like field name plus the blocking/preferred columns country and industry. Avoid Sales and assets because they have very high null rates on the dbpedia side, making them unreliable. Jaccard suits short entity/country names, while cosine is safer for industry because label granularity may differ substantially across sources.

Use all blocking columns but shift emphasis strongly to name with jaro_winkler for short company-name variations/typos. Country is low-null and useful as a light constraint. Industry is noisy and partially missing on the right, so keep lower weight and use cosine to tolerate wording differences. Avoid Sales/assets due to very high null rates in dbpedia. Lower threshold from prior failed setup because cross-source labels are noisy.

Use all blocking columns but change similarity choices: name is the strongest signal and benefits from jaro_winkler for short company names with spelling/format variation; country is low-null and categorical, so jaccard is appropriate; industry is noisier and more heterogeneous in DBpedia, so cosine is more tolerant of wording differences. Avoid highly sparse numeric columns. Lower threshold slightly from prior failed 0.75 runs to improve recall while keeping precision via strong name weighting.

Use all blocking columns but change comparators to better fit the data: name is a short entity string where Jaro-Winkler handles spelling variations well; country is a short categorical field suited to Jaccard; industry appears noisier and more heterogeneous across sources, so cosine is more forgiving than exact token overlap. Avoid sparse numeric fields (Sales, assets) due to very high null rates on the right table. Lower the threshold slightly because prior attempts with the same columns missed matches just below 0.75.

Use the two shared blocking columns only. Name is the most informative and complete field, so it gets the highest weight. Country is useful as a supporting constraint but has substantial nulls on the right side, so it gets lower weight. Both are short categorical/entity strings, making jaccard with lower/strip normalization a good rule-based choice.

Use strong name matching plus blocking fields country and city, and founded as a supportive date signal. Name is complete and highly informative, so jaro_winkler fits short organization names with minor variations. Country and city are useful scalar location fields despite some nulls on the left. Founded appears date-like from samples, but has higher nulls, so it gets moderate weight and a tolerant 730-day window for possible source/date granularity differences.

Use all informative shared scalar columns while keeping strong emphasis on name. Switch name and city to jaro_winkler to better handle spelling variation/accents in short entity and location names. Keep blocking columns represented. Founded appears date-like despite string dtype, but has high nulls on the left, so give it modest weight and allow a wider 2-year tolerance for edition/record differences. Lower the threshold slightly since the previous configuration with these columns was just below the target.

Use all informative scalar common columns while changing the name comparator to jaro_winkler for short organization names with spelling variation/diacritics. Keep blocking columns represented, downweight noisier city and sparse founded, and use a slightly lower threshold because prior attempts with the same columns were just below target.

Use name as the strongest signal with jaro_winkler for short organization names and minor spelling variations, while including blocking columns country and city with lighter weights. Treat founded as a date with a 2-year tolerance to handle incomplete or shifted founding dates. Raise threshold slightly above prior failed runs and rebalance weights toward the most reliable identifier.

Use the blocking columns with strongest emphasis on name, switching to jaro_winkler for short organization/place names to better handle typos and spelling variation. Keep country and city as supportive signals, and include founded as a low-weight date feature with a wider tolerance for DBpedia/fullcontact date variation. Lower threshold slightly because prior attempts with the same columns were just below target and founded has high nulls on the left.
